title: taraxacum alaid-montanum
intro: Taraxacum alaid-montanum, also known as the alaid mountain dandelion, is a rare species of flowering plant in the dandelion genus. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it is a perennial herb used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing symptoms of gastrointestinal issues, improving liver function, and aiding in the treatment of jaundice and other liver-related conditions. Taraxacum alaid-montanum is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as tea or infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water.
